elo_title: Introduction to the Subject : Data is gathered information
elo_description: especially facts or numbers - that can be used to find out things or to make decisions when examined. Data handling is the process of gathering, recording, and presenting the data or information in a way that is meani ngful t o others. Good questions are an integral part of data collection. As well, data collection provides a way to connect mathematics to other su bject areas. The data collected can then be analysed to make data -driven decisions in the real world.

subject: science
elo_description: Plan and conduct investigations to provide evidence that vibrating materials can make sound and that sound can make materials vibrate. Students explore how sounds are produced, classify sounds by loudness, and investigate the effects of sounds on objects. They learn that sound is a wave and understand the role of vibrations in creating sound.
grade_expectations: Students will demonstrate understanding of how to produce sounds, compare and describe sounds as loud, soft, pleasant, or unpleasant, and discuss the effects and uses of sounds in daily life.

subject: mathematics
elo_description: learners will meaningfully sequence and use a range of counting and grouping strategies.

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Understanding What and How We Measure
elo_description: Developing an understanding of measurable attributes.
grade_expectations: Students will explore physical materials and describe them using vocabulary associated with length (short/long, high/low, near/far, thick/thin, narrow/wide, deep/shallow), capacity (full, empty, same as), mass (heavy/light), and area (large/small).

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Learners will recognise, represent and partition whole numbers up to 20
elo_description: frames and created materials. ● Model a given number up to 20 using a variety of pictorial representation. ● Find examples of a given number in the environment . ● place given numerals on a number line (vertical and horizontal) with benchmarks 0, 5, 10, 15 and 20 . Observation - Concrete : Observe learners as they work with concrete materials. Are they able to represent numbers accurately? - Check to see if learners are making use of resources correctly. • Pictorial : Observe students as they make pictorial representation of numbers. Have them explain representations. Count the stars, then colour the same amount in the ten frames. Students should begin with representing and partitioning quantities to 5, then 10, then 20. Using the 10 -frame with two -color counters allows students to build and recognize relationships to 5, 10 and 20. For example, 8 is 2 less than 10. Have students represent given numbers using bottle caps, counters, sticks, seashells, stones, marbles, dots, magnetic numbers, fingers, blocks or any available material in their environment. <!-- PAGE 12 -->

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Students will be able to demonstrate their understanding of numbers and their values by comparing and ordering

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Learner’s will comprehend the importance of the number ten and its role when counting from eleven to nineteen, as well as
elo_description: Allow children to count teen numbers using tens and ones. Observation ❖ Pictorial - Introduce The Number Slide. The Number Slide transforms the expanded form numbers (e.g. , 10 1, 10 2, etc.) into the conventional number (e.g. 11, 12, etc.). The students will: Students should be given opportunities to make and display sets of tens and ones in a number of ways (E.g., using whole number rods) Visualize and interact with teen numbers as ten and “some ” more . <!-- PAGE 29 -->

subject: science
elo_description: Use tools and materials to design and build devices that use light or sound to solve the problem of communicating over a distance. Students explore how light and sound can travel over distances and identify simple communication devices. They design, build, test, and evaluate communication devices while understanding how people use light and sound to communicate.
grade_expectations: Students will identify and describe simple communication devices, design and build a device that uses light or sound to communicate, and describe ways people use devices that utilize light and sound for communication.

subject: science
elo_description: Observe and describe the effects of sunlight on different materials. Students compare the warming effects of sunlight on shaded and unshaded areas, test the effectiveness of shade structures, and design and build structures to provide shade and reduce heat from sunlight. They understand that sunlight warms Earth's surface.
grade_expectations: Students will observe and describe how sunlight affects different materials, compare shaded and unshaded areas, and design solutions to reduce the warming effects of sunlight.

subject: science
elo_description: Understand the relationship between living things and their environment. Students observe and describe how living things get what they need to survive, recognize that humans are animals with similar needs, and identify the basic needs of plants (water, light, air, nutrients) and animals (food, water, air, shelter).
grade_expectations: Students will identify and describe the basic needs of plants and animals, understand how living things obtain what they need from their surroundings, and recognize the connection between living things and their environment.

subject: science
elo_description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that young plants and animals are like, but not exactly like, their parents. Students recognize that individuals of the same kind vary in their characteristics, compare young and adult plants and animals to identify similarities and differences, and understand that offspring inherit characteristics from their parents.
grade_expectations: Students will observe and describe characteristics of adult and young plants and animals, compare them to identify similarities and differences, and understand that offspring resemble but are not identical to their parents.

subject: science
elo_description: Read texts and use media to determine patterns in behavior of parents and offspring that help offspring survive. Students compare parental care behaviors in different animals, describe behaviors that help young animals survive, identify ways that parents care for their offspring, observe patterns of parental care across species, and understand how parental behaviors increase offspring survival.
grade_expectations: Students will identify and describe parental care behaviors, compare how different animals care for their young, and understand the connection between parental care and offspring survival.

subject: science
elo_description: Understand that living things live where their needs are met and that changes to habitats can affect the living things that live there. Students identify different habitats (forest, ocean, desert, wetland, etc.), describe how different habitats meet the needs of living things, match plants and animals to their appropriate habitats, and create simple models showing relationships between living things and their habitats.
grade_expectations: Students will identify different types of habitats, describe how habitats meet the needs of living things, match organisms to appropriate habitats, and understand that habitat changes affect living things.

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Understanding What and How We Measure
elo_description: Comparing and ordering based on measurable attributes.
grade_expectations: Students will compare and order objects based on measurable attributes of length, capacity, mass, and area using appropriate vocabulary (longer/shorter, taller/shorter, holds more/less, heavier/lighter, larger/smaller area).

subject: mathematics
elo_description: Learners will understand the meaning of addition and subtraction and how they relate.

subject: mathematics
elo_description: Compute Fluently

subject: mathematics
elo_description: Describe, create, extend and generalise patterns

subject: mathematics
elo_description: Representing Unknowns

subject: science
elo_description: Plan and conduct investigations to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties. Students identify and describe properties of materials including color, texture, hardness, and flexibility. They analyze data from testing different materials to determine which properties are best suited for an intended purpose. Students observe and describe reversible and irreversible changes in materials, demonstrate that heating or cooling can cause changes, and make observations about how objects can be disassembled and reassembled.
grade_expectations: Students will classify materials based on their properties, test different materials to determine suitability for specific purposes, communicate findings about material properties, understand that different properties are suited to different purposes, and observe both reversible and irreversible changes in materials.

subject: science
elo_description: Plan and conduct investigations to determine if plants need sunlight and water to grow. Students make observations of plants and animals to compare diversity of life in different habitats, develop simple models that mimic how animals disperse seeds or pollinate plants, describe how living things depend on each other and their environment, and observe and record the life cycle of plants. They understand that some animals migrate or hibernate in response to seasonal changes, compare how different plants and animals adapt to their habitats, and recognize that plants depend on animals for pollination and seed dispersal.
grade_expectations: Students will identify different habitats and the living things found in them, understand that animals depend on their surroundings to get what they need, recognize plant-animal interdependence, plan investigations about plant needs, compare how organisms adapt to different habitats, and understand seasonal adaptations in animals.

subject: science
elo_description: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time. Students record and analyze weather data including temperature, precipitation, and wind. They recognize that some places are hotter or colder than others and relate this to location, and identify seasonal patterns in weather and how they affect living things.
grade_expectations: Students will record and analyze weather data, identify seasonal patterns, recognize geographic temperature variations, and understand how weather patterns affect living things.

subject: science
elo_description: Obtain information to identify where water is found on Earth and understand that water can be solid or liquid. Students understand that water is found in oceans, rivers, lakes, and ponds. They develop models to represent the shapes and kinds of land and bodies of water in an area, observe and describe the water cycle in simple terms, recognize that water can change between solid and liquid forms, and compare the effects of different amounts of water, light, or air on plant growth.
grade_expectations: Students will identify where water is found on Earth, understand water exists in different states, describe the water cycle simply, develop models of landforms and water bodies, and recognize how water affects plant growth.

subject: science
elo_description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation to define a simple problem that can be solved through development of a new or improved object or tool. Students develop simple sketches, drawings, or physical models to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a problem. They analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare strengths and weaknesses, design and build structures to solve problems, test and evaluate solutions making improvements as needed, use appropriate tools and materials to build models and prototypes, communicate design ideas and solutions through drawings and descriptions, and understand that there are many possible solutions to design problems.
grade_expectations: Students will identify problems that need solutions, communicate design ideas clearly, develop models to illustrate solutions, analyze and compare different design solutions, test and improve designs iteratively, and use appropriate tools for building prototypes.

subject: science
elo_description: Plan and conduct an investigation to compare the effects of different strengths or different directions of pushes and pulls on the motion of an object. Students observe and describe the motion of objects, investigate how forces affect the movement of objects, understand that pushes and pulls can have different strengths and directions, recognize that pushing or pulling on an object can change its speed or direction, make predictions about how forces will affect object motion, analyze data to determine if a design solution works to change the speed or direction of an object, and test different designs to solve problems involving motion.
grade_expectations: Students will observe and describe object motion, investigate how forces affect movement, understand that pushes and pulls vary in strength and direction, recognize how forces change object speed or direction, make predictions about force effects, and test design solutions for motion problems.

subject: science
elo_description: Demonstrate curiosity about the natural world through questioning and exploration. Students ask questions and make predictions based on observations, plan and conduct simple investigations with guidance, make careful observations and measurements using simple tools, record observations using drawings, numbers, and written descriptions, compare results with predictions and draw simple conclusions, communicate findings through presentations, models, and reports, work respectfully and safely with others during investigations, and follow safety procedures when conducting experiments.
grade_expectations: Students will demonstrate scientific curiosity through questioning, ask questions and make predictions, plan and conduct investigations, make observations using tools, record data in multiple formats, compare results with predictions, communicate findings effectively, and work collaboratively and safely during investigations.

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Understanding What and How We Measure
elo_description: Developing and applying non-standard units of measure.
grade_expectations: Students will use non-standard units (hand spans, footsteps, cubes, paper clips) to measure length, capacity, mass, and area of objects. They will understand that different units give different measurements.

subject: mathematics
elo_title: Understanding What and How We Measure
elo_description: Developing and applying standard units of measure.
grade_expectations: Students will be introduced to standard units of measurement for length (centimeters, meters) and mass (grams, kilograms). They will understand why standard units are useful for consistent measurement.
